strengths: Clearly identifies three specific objects and provides adequate real-world context for each.
Consistently establishes explicit links between every object and the chosen IA prompt, using TOK concepts of knowledge, belief and opinion.
Most claims are supported with concrete historical or conceptual evidence (e.g., Platoâs definition of knowledge, All-India Radio ban example).
Effective use of a well-credited photograph of the Konark wheel to ground the discussion in reality.
Historical account of the harmoniumâs ban and reinstatement vividly illustrates the dynamism of opinion in a sociopolitical context.
weaknesses: Occasional sweeping assertions (e.g., universality of mathematical axioms, immutability of belief) lack necessary qualification or scholarly support.
Incomplete references and interrupted sentences (cut-offs) detract from clarity and precision.
Repetition of definitions across sections and descriptive passages dilute analytical sharpness.
Counter-perspectives (e.g., Gettier problems, empirical critiques) are only briefly acknowledged, limiting depth and balance.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the descriptors for the 7â8 band by identifying three specific objects, grounding them in clear real-world contexts, and making well-supported links to the IA prompt on knowledge, belief and opinion. The student draws effectively on historical and conceptual evidence, demonstrating a solid grasp of TOK principles in lived realities. However, the analysis lacks precision in placesâsweeping assertions, incomplete references, interrupted sentences and limited engagement with counter-perspectives prevent it from reaching the consistently sophisticated level required for the top band (9â10). On balance, the work sits at the lower end of the 7â8 band, warranting a score of 7.

annotations.comment: Inclusion of the Raj journals article by Rahaim demonstrates effective use of a peer-reviewed source to underpin the harmoniumâs historical context.

The introduction outlines the aim but lacks a clear roadmap showing how each object will address the criteria. Consider stating explicitly how the analysis of each object will distinguish knowledge, belief and opinion to guide the reader.
Clearly stating the IA prompt at the outset anchors the entire exhibition. This focus aids the reader in understanding the central question being addressed.
Effective use of a specific, well-credited photograph of the Konark wheel helps ground the discussion in a real-world context and strengthens the link to the prompt.

This descriptive paragraph captures the artifactâs physical features but does not yet connect these details to distinctions among knowledge, belief and opinion. Introduce TOK terminology here to link form to function.

The claim that mathematical knowledge is universal and certain needs qualification. Consider acknowledging potential limitations (e.g., axiomatic choices) or referencing epistemological critiques such as Gettier scenarios to add precision.
The citation for Platoâs Theaetetus lacks publication details or translation information. Use a standard academic format including translator, edition and page numbers to enhance credibility.
The website reference for the Konark wheel is non-academic and lacks author or date metadata. Consider supplementing with a peer-reviewed or scholarly source for stronger justification.
Drawing on observable phenomena to justify the wheelâs reliability demonstrates a sound link between empirical evidence and the concept of knowledge. This is an effective TOK connection.
The discussion of verifiability would benefit from explicit reference to TOK theories of justification (e.g., correspondence vs coherence). Adding such terminology would deepen the analysis.
Consider adding a brief concluding sentence for Object 1 before transitioning to Object 2 to reinforce how the wheel exemplifies knowledge under the chosen criteria.
The origin of the Sphatika mala is asserted but not substantiated by historical scholarship. A specific ethnographic or academic reference would strengthen the claim of cultural credibility.
This sentence is cut off mid-thought, interrupting clarity. Complete the explanation to ensure the justification for classifying the mala as belief is fully articulated.
Relying on a commercial Etsy listing as evidence for historical beliefs is tenuous. Replace or supplement with scholarly sources on crystal healing traditions.
The assertion that beliefs cannot be rationally challenged is too absolute. Acknowledge that beliefs can be examined through critical inquiry or philosophical debate for balance.
The personal example adds depth but lacks explicit linkage back to the TOK prompt. State clearly how this illustrates the nature and limits of belief versus opinion.
The historical account of the harmoniumâs ban and reinstatement provides concrete evidence of shifting opinions, effectively linking real-world context to the concept of opinion.
This sentence ends abruptly and fails to define âopinionâ fully. Complete the definition and tie it explicitly to the harmonium example for clarity.
The DhamVasis website is a commercial vendor; consider citing a musicology text or historical archive to support claims about the instrumentâs origins.
The definition of opinion would benefit from integration of TOK terminology such as âWays of Knowingâ or reference to scholarship on subjectivity to deepen conceptual precision.
Effective illustration of the dynamism of opinions via the harmoniumâs reinstatement on All India Radio underscores how sociopolitical context shapes subjective judgments.

The conclusion summarizes criteria but does not revisit how each object concretely exemplifies distinctions among knowledge, belief and opinion. Consider a brief synthesis referencing all three artifacts.
References are inconsistently formatted (e.g., some include DOIs, others donât). Adopt a single citation style for coherence and academic rigor.
There is noticeable repetition in defining terms across sections. Streamline definitions and avoid redundancy to maintain analytical sharpness.
To deepen the epistemological analysis, consider incorporating modern critiques such as the Gettier problem when discussing âjustified, true beliefâ.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
